The Miracle of Dunkirk successfully rescues over 338,000 Allied soldiers stranded on the beaches of France.
The Berlin Airlift begins, rescuing the blockaded city from starvation by constantly flying in supplies.
Operation Entebbe successfully rescues over 100 hostages held at an airport in Uganda.
The Canadian Caper successfully smuggles six American diplomats out of Iran.
US Air Force Captain Scott O'Grady is rescued by Marines after being shot down over Bosnia.
The dramatic rescue of the 33 trapped Chilean miners captivates millions on live television.
The Tham Luang cave rescue operation successfully extracts a boys' soccer team in Thailand.
McDonald's – Founded by Richard and Maurice McDonald in San Bernardino
In-N-Out Burger – California's first drive-thru hamburger stand opens
Burger King – Founded in Miami, originally as Insta-Burger King
Taco Bell – Glen Bell opens the first location in California
Subway – Opened originally as 'Pete\'s Super Submarines'
Wendy's – Dave Thomas opens the first restaurant in Ohio
Starbucks – The first store opens at Seattle's Pike Place Market
First YouTube video uploaded
Windows 1.0 released
First commercial cellular network (1G)
Nintendo Entertainment System released in US
First commercial microprocessor (Intel 4004)
First iPhone announced
Sony PlayStation released
First Network Email – Ray Tomlinson sends the first message using the '@' symbol
First Domain Registered – 'symbolics.com' becomes the first registered .com domain
World Wide Web Invented – Tim Berners-Lee writes the initial proposal at CERN
First Webcam – Created at Cambridge University just to monitor a coffee pot
First eBay Sale – Founder Pierre Omidyar sells a broken laser pointer
First Wikipedia Article – The crowdsourced encyclopedia officially launches
First Bitcoin Transaction – Satoshi Nakamoto sends 10 BTC to Hal Finney

category: The Golden Age of Game Show Premieres

events.event: The Price Is Right premieres on NBC with original host Bill Cullen.
Let's Make a Deal premieres, hosted by Monty Hall.
Jeopardy! makes its television debut with original host Art Fleming.
Wheel of Fortune premieres on daytime television, created by Merv Griffin.
Family Feud debuts on ABC, hosted by Richard Dawson.
Press Your Luck premieres, introducing audiences to the dreaded Whammy.
Who Wants to Be a Millionaire? debuts in the US with Regis Philbin.
The massive quiz show scandals rock television, leading to congressional hearings.
Bob Barker begins his legendary 35-year run hosting the revival of The Price Is Right.
Vanna White joins Wheel of Fortune as the regular co-host.
Michael Larson famously memorizes the board pattern to win big on Press Your Luck.
Supermarket Sweep is revived on Lifetime, quickly becoming a daytime cult classic.
Ken Jennings begins his record-breaking 74-game winning streak on Jeopardy!
Drew Carey succeeds Bob Barker as the host of The Price Is Right.
Bob Dylan – born Robert Zimmerman
Freddie Mercury – born Farrokh Bulsara
Stevie Wonder – born Stevland Hardaway Judkins
Shania Twain – born Eilleen Regina Edwards
Katy Perry – born Katheryn Elizabeth Hudson
Lady Gaga – born Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta
The Weeknd – born Abel Makkonen Tesfaye
Coca-Cola invented
Microsoft founded
Disney founded
Bitcoin network created
Walmart founded
Google incorporated
Tesla Motors incorporated
First successful airplane flight by Wright brothers
Sputnik 1 launched
Magna Carta signed
Declaration of Independence signed
End of World War II
Chernobyl disaster
Nelson Mandela released from prison
Leave It to Beaver premieres, epitomizing the idealized mid-century American nuclear family.
The Brady Bunch debuts, defining the blended family sitcom for a generation.
The Cosby Show premieres, revitalizing the family sitcom format on NBC.
The Simpsons debuts as a half-hour series, satirizing the traditional family unit.
Everybody Loves Raymond premieres, mining comedy from overbearing extended family.
Malcolm in the Middle debuts, offering a chaotic, single-camera look at family life.
Black-ish premieres, exploring a family's sociopolitical and cultural identity.
Hewlett-Packard (HP) is founded in a small Palo Alto garage.
Intel is founded by Gordon Moore and Robert Noyce to build semiconductor memory.
Microsoft is founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen to develop software for the Altair 8800.
Apple is founded by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak to sell the Apple I personal computer.
Amazon is founded by Jeff Bezos in his garage as an online marketplace for books.
Google is founded by Larry Page and Sergey Brin while they are PhD students at Stanford.
Facebook (Meta) is founded by Mark Zuckerberg at Harvard University.
Nadia Comăneci scores perfect 10
Babe Ruth hits 60 home runs
Muhammad Ali lights Olympic cauldron
Roger Bannister breaks 4-minute mile

Jackie Robinson breaks baseball color barrier

Burj Khalifa opened
Eiffel Tower completed

Colosseum completed

Oreo – Nabisco introduces the 'Oreo Biscuit' to the market
Ritz Crackers – Introduced as a buttery, circular cracker during the Great Depression
Cheetos – Invented by Fritos creator Charles Elmer Doolin
Pop-Tarts – Kellogg's introduces the legendary toaster pastry
Doritos – Originally created at a restaurant in Disneyland and released nationwide
Pringles – Procter & Gamble introduces 'Pringle\'s Newfangled Potato Chips'
Lunchables – Oscar Mayer launches the pre-packaged, build-your-own lunch
IBM is founded originally as the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company.
Intel is incorporated, kickstarting the Silicon Valley microprocessor revolution.
Oracle is founded by Larry Ellison to build commercial relational database systems.
Netflix is founded by Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph as a DVD-by-mail service.
YouTube is founded by three former PayPal employees.
Twitter is founded, introducing the concept of microblogging in 140 characters.
Uber is founded as a black-car service called Ubercab.
Superman – Debuts in Action Comics #1
Batman – Debuts in Detective Comics #27
Wonder Woman – Debuts in All Star Comics #8
Spider-Man – Debuts in Amazing Fantasy #15
Wolverine – Debuts in a cameo in The Incredible Hulk #180
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles – Debuts in a self-published comic
Deadpool – Debuts in The New Mutants #98
Sir Ernest Shackleton's stranded crew is finally rescued from Elephant Island.
The crew of Apollo 13 is safely brought back to Earth after a critical explosion in space.
The 16 survivors of a plane crash in the freezing Andes mountains are rescued after 72 days.
Baby Jessica McClure is safely pulled from an abandoned well in Texas after a 58-hour ordeal.
All nine miners trapped in the flooded Quecreek Mine in Pennsylvania are successfully rescued.
Captain Richard Phillips is rescued from Somali pirates by US Navy SEAL snipers.
Four indigenous children are rescued after surviving 40 days in the Amazon rainforest following a plane crash.

Hawaii Five-O premieres, bringing high-stakes police procedural action to the tropical islands.
Columbo debuts as a regular series, popularizing the 'howcatchem' inverted detective format.
Murder, She Wrote premieres, starring Angela Lansbury as the mystery-solving author Jessica Fletcher.
Law & Order debuts, defining the modern two-part police and legal procedural format.
CSI: Crime Scene Investigation premieres, heavily influencing the depiction of forensic science in pop culture.
NCIS debuts, eventually growing into one of the most-watched procedural franchises in the world.
House M.D. premieres, applying the Sherlock Holmes detective formula to rare medical diagnoses.
Jim Morrison is arrested for indecent exposure and profanity during a Doors concert in Miami.
Paul McCartney is arrested in Tokyo after customs officials find half a pound of marijuana in his luggage.
Paul Reubens (Pee-wee Herman) is arrested for indecent exposure in a Florida adult movie theater.
O.J. Simpson is arrested following the infamous, televised low-speed white Ford Bronco chase.
Matthew McConaughey is arrested in Texas for marijuana possession and resisting arrest while allegedly playing bongo drums naked.
Nick Nolte is arrested for a DUI, resulting in arguably the most chaotic and iconic celebrity mugshot of all time.
Bruno Mars is arrested in Las Vegas after a bathroom attendant catches him with cocaine.

Game of Thrones premieres on HBO, bringing unprecedented scale and budget to high fantasy television.
Stranger Things debuts on Netflix, creating a massive phenomenon out of sci-fi horror and 1980s nostalgia.
Severance premieres, exploring a dystopian sci-fi world where work and personal memories are surgically divided.

Bill Gates is arrested in New Mexico for a traffic violation, resulting in a famously cheerful mugshot.
O.J. Simpson is arrested following the infamous, televised low-speed white Ford Bronco chase.
Hugh Grant is arrested in Los Angeles for lewd conduct in a public place.
Winona Ryder is arrested for shoplifting thousands of dollars of merchandise at Saks Fifth Avenue in Beverly Hills.
Lindsay Lohan is arrested for a DUI, marking the beginning of highly publicized legal troubles.
Justin Bieber is arrested in Miami for a DUI and illegal drag racing.
Jim Morrison is arrested for indecent exposure and profanity during a Doors concert in Miami.

Happy Days premieres, centering around Richie Cunningham, Fonzie, and their friends at Arnold's Drive-In.
The Golden Girls debuts, proving that a lively friend group sitcom works perfectly for women in their 50s and 60s.
Friends premieres on NBC, launching its six stars to global fame from the Central Perk coffee shop.
That '70s Show debuts, following a group of teenage friends hanging out in a Wisconsin basement.
How I Met Your Mother premieres, tracking a tight-knit friend group in NYC through MacLaren's Pub.
New Girl debuts, exploring the chaotic dynamic of four roommates sharing an LA loft.
Broad City premieres, chronicling the daily misadventures of two best friends in New York City.
The Beverly Hillbillies premieres, following a poor family who strikes oil and moves to a wealthy California mansion.
Green Acres debuts, featuring a wealthy New York City couple attempting to live as farmers.
Diff'rent Strokes premieres, following two boys from Harlem taken in by a wealthy Park Avenue businessman.
Perfect Strangers debuts, exploring the culture clash between a midwesterner and his distant Mediterranean cousin.
The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air premieres, starring Will Smith as a street-smart teen sent to live with wealthy relatives.
Schitt's Creek debuts, stranding a formerly filthy-rich family in a run-down rural town.
Ted Lasso premieres, following an optimistic American football coach hired to manage an English soccer team.
Bewitched premieres, following a powerful witch who marries an ordinary mortal man.
I Dream of Jeannie debuts, focusing on a 2,000-year-old genie and the astronaut who rescues her.
ALF premieres, following an alien from the planet Melmac who crashes into a suburban family's garage.
3rd Rock from the Sun debuts, featuring a group of extraterrestrials posing as a human family on Earth.
The Good Place premieres, twisting philosophy and the afterlife into a bright, colorful sitcom format.
Ghosts (Original UK Series) debuts, following a young couple who inherit a haunted country house.
WandaVision premieres, trapping two Marvel superheroes inside evolving eras of classic television sitcoms.

category: The Satirical / 'No Hugging, No Learning' Sitcom

events.event: Married... with Children premieres, providing a raunchy, cynical counter-programming option to wholesome family sitcoms.
Seinfeld debuts as The Seinfeld Chronicles, famously championing a 'no hugging, no learning' mantra.
The Larry Sanders Show premieres on HBO, offering a dark, satirical behind-the-scenes look at a late-night talk show.
Curb Your Enthusiasm debuts, turning minor social grievances into massively awkward disasters.
Arrested Development premieres, chronicling the highly selfish, out-of-touch Bluth family.
It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia debuts, pushing the boundaries of terrible behavior among a group of pub owners.
Veep premieres on HBO, providing a razor-sharp, profane satire of American politics.
Trailer Park Boys premieres, utilizing a raw documentary style to follow the residents of Sunnyvale Trailer Park.
Reno 911! debuts, spoofing the format of the documentary reality show COPS.
The Office (US) premieres on NBC, popularizing the workplace mockumentary format for American audiences.
Modern Family debuts, utilizing the mockumentary style to capture the dynamics of a large extended family.
People Just Do Nothing premieres on the BBC, following the operators of a pirate radio station.
What We Do in the Shadows debuts, applying the mockumentary format to a house of ancient vampires in Staten Island.
Abbott Elementary premieres, following a documentary crew filming underfunded teachers in Philadelphia.
Dallas premieres, captivating audiences with oil tycoon wealth and the 'Who shot J.R.?' phenomenon.
Dynasty debuts, offering a wildly dramatic, campy look at wealthy rival families in Colorado.
Melrose Place premieres, turning a Los Angeles apartment complex into a hub of betrayal and manipulation.
Grey's Anatomy debuts, blending high-stakes medical cases with intense romantic melodrama.
Scandal premieres, delivering fast-paced, addictive political melodrama in Washington D.C.
This Is Us debuts, utilizing multiple timelines to deliver highly emotional, tear-jerking family drama.
Yellowstone premieres, reviving the prime-time soap opera format through the lens of a modern Western ranch.
Roots premieres, a landmark historical miniseries that gripped the entire nation.
Band of Brothers debuts on HBO, offering a harrowing, cinematic look at WWII.
Rome premieres, setting a new standard for high-budget historical epics on television.
Downton Abbey debuts, exploring the rigid class structures of a post-Edwardian English country estate.
Peaky Blinders premieres, stylizing the violent post-WWI criminal underworld of Birmingham.
The Crown debuts on Netflix, chronically the decades-long reign of Queen Elizabeth II.
Chernobyl premieres, meticulously dramatizing the catastrophic 1986 nuclear disaster.
The West Wing premieres, offering an idealized, fast-talking look at the inner workings of the White House.
24 debuts, revolutionizing the espionage thriller with its real-time ticking clock format.
Damages premieres, presenting a dark, ruthless take on high-stakes corporate litigation.
The Good Wife debuts, wrapping complex legal and political maneuvering inside a network procedural format.
Homeland premieres, delivering tense, psychological espionage involving the CIA and terrorism.
House of Cards debuts, following a deeply corrupt politician's ruthless climb to the presidency.
Billions premieres, exploring the high-stakes financial warfare between a hedge fund king and a US Attorney.
Beverly Hills, 90210 premieres, defining the modern teen drama genre for the entire decade.
My So-Called Life debuts, offering a deeply realistic and critically acclaimed look at teenage angst.
Dawson's Creek premieres, famous for its highly articulate teenagers navigating complex relationships.
The O.C. debuts, bringing indie rock and wealthy California drama to the forefront of pop culture.
Friday Night Lights premieres, offering a grounded, documentary-style look at Texas high school football.
Gossip Girl debuts, heavily influencing late 2000s teen fashion and internet culture.
Euphoria premieres on HBO, providing a visually stunning, highly explicit look at modern teenage trauma.

Universal Studios Florida – Universal opens a theme park to rival Disney in Orlando
Disney's Animal Kingdom – Disney blends a zoo with high-tech theme park rides
The Wizarding World of Harry Potter – The immersive Hogsmeade land opens at Universal
Ford Model T – Henry Ford introduces the first affordable, mass-produced automobile
Volkswagen Beetle – Production begins on the iconic 'Bug'
Chevrolet Corvette – The quintessential American sports car is introduced
Ford Mustang – The original 'pony car' makes a wildly successful debut
Toyota Prius – The world's first mass-produced hybrid passenger car goes on sale
Tesla Model S – Tesla releases its revolutionary luxury all-electric sedan
Ford F-150 Lightning – Ford launches the all-electric version of its best-selling truck
The Microwave Oven – Percy Spencer accidentally discovers microwave cooking using radar tech
The Credit Card – Diners Club introduces the first modern multipurpose charge card
The Barcode – Norman Joseph Woodland and Bernard Silver receive the patent
The Computer Mouse – Douglas Engelbart publicly demonstrates the device for the first time
The Digital Camera – Steven Sasson at Kodak creates the first digital camera
The Compact Disc (CD) – Commercially released by Sony and Philips
The Smartphone – The IBM Simon goes on sale, featuring apps and a touchscreen
